title: candollea pygmaea
intro: Candollea pygmaea, also known as dwarf candollea, is a small shrub native to tropical Africa, characterized by its compact growth habit and white, star-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Candollea pygmaea is believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, and its leaves and roots are dried and crushed to make a powder for medicinal use.
